long_term.what_would_change_view: Long-term conviction would improve if subsequent quarters sustain or accelerate the Q1-2026 growth rate enough to lift the Growth score materially above 21, or if stability and dividend pillars strengthen further; it would deteriorate if growth proves one-off or geopolitical escalation erodes the diversified holding model.

valuation_note: The available streams do not include a current price or forward multiple; mid-May coverage noted the stock at د.إ3.38, but without fresh data the only structural supports are the Dividend score of 61 and the Q1 growth snapshot, weighed against a weak Growth score of 21.
dominant_factor: news — the most recent dated company-specific signals (positive disclosure tone and Q1-2026 results, 29-44 days old) are the freshest concrete inputs and drive the short-term read, outweighing older technicals and generic sector geopolitics that lack direct company linkage.
signal_alignment: The streams partially contradict. The news and short-signal tone skew positive, while the house growth score (21/100), long-signal weak-growth flag, and negative price momentum (-1.6%) point the other way; elevated sector geopolitical risk adds a headwind without company-specific corroboration.

scenarios.bull: Sustained execution on Q1-2026 growth rates lifts the growth score materially and allows re-rating despite elevated geopolitical noise.
scenarios.base: Mixed fundamentals and stale news flow keep the stock in a Neutral (low) range, with neither the positive disclosure narrative nor the weak growth score decisively taking control.
scenarios.bear: Negative price momentum persists, growth fails to sustain beyond the Q1 benchmark, and sector geopolitical stress weighs on the holding structure.
